text: Iron catalysis was discovered to achieve the intermolecular 1,2‐addition of perfluoroalkyl iodides to alkynes and alkenes in an efficient manner. The iron catalysis was demonstrated to be highly compatible with diverse substrates and functions. A wide range of perfluoroalkyl iodides, including the simplest one CF 3 I, can be applied. The obtained perfluoroalkylated alkyl and alkenyl iodides can be further modified through cross‐coupling reactions, thus enabling an efficient construction of diverse perfluoroalkylated organic molecules.
explanation: The text is highly polished with generic, non-specific claims and lacks detailed experimental data; this pattern is common in AI-generated abstracts.
